title: phlegmariurus hartwegianus
intro: Phlegmariurus hartwegianus, also known as the Hartweg's climbing fern or Hartweg's forked polypody, is a species of epiphytic fern in the family Lycopodiaceae. Traditionally used in herbal medicine, Phlegmariurus hartwegianus is believed to possess medicinal properties that help in reducing inflammation and treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health, as well as its ability to act as an anti-inflammatory agent. In herbal preparations, Phlegmariurus hartwegianus is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are then consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
